task_completion.description: The document contains a centered 'References' heading at the top.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A word processor window showing the word 'References' at the very top of the document, aligned to the center of the page.

task_completion.description: The 10 citations are sorted alphabetically by the first author's last name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The list of citations appears in this exact order by first author: Bandura, Bowlby, Erikson, Kahneman, Maslow, Milgram, Pavlov, Piaget, Skinner, Vygotsky.

task_completion.description: All citation entries utilize a 0.5-inch hanging indent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The first line of each citation is flush with the left margin, and all subsequent lines of the same citation are indented to the right (typically visible on the top ruler as a 0.5-inch offset for the bottom triangle).

task_completion.description: Authors are formatted correctly (Lastname, Initials) and publication years are enclosed in parentheses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Entries begin with author names formatted like 'Bandura, A.' or 'Kahneman, D., & Tversky, A.' followed immediately by the year in parentheses, such as '(1977).' or '(1979).'.

task_completion.description: Article titles are in sentence case, and publication titles (journals or book titles) are italicized.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Article titles (e.g., 'Prospect theory', 'Behavioral study of obedience') are in sentence case (only first word and proper nouns capitalized). Journal names (e.g., 'Econometrica') and book titles (e.g., 'Social learning theory', 'The behavior of organisms') are italicized.

task_completion.description: The updated document is saved to the original path (/home/ga/Documents/raw_citations.docx).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The word processor title bar shows 'raw_citations.docx' without an 'unsaved' indicator, or a terminal/file explorer window confirms the file was saved/overwritten at the correct path.

integrity.description: The agent used a word processor GUI (e.g., LibreOffice Writer) to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a graphical word processor application open and being interacted with, rather than terminal commands or a plain text editor (like nano/vim) modifying the raw XML of the .docx file.

integrity.description: The hanging indent was applied using paragraph formatting tools, not by manually typing spaces or tabs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The ruler or paragraph settings dialog in the GUI shows a hanging indent configuration. There should be no jagged alignments or visible space/tab characters (if formatting marks are enabled) used to fake the indent.

integrity.description: The agent formatted the existing text rather than pasting a pre-generated perfect list from an external source.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the agent manipulating the existing text, applying styles, and editing individual entries, rather than a single paste action replacing the whole document with a perfectly formatted list.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The document must contain exactly 10 citations. When sorted alphabetically, the expected first authors are: Bandura, Bowlby, Erikson, Kahneman, Maslow, Milgram, Pavlov, Piaget, Skinner, and Vygotsky. Specific works include Kahneman & Tversky's 'Prospect Theory' (1979, Econometrica), Bandura's 'Social Learning Theory' (1977), Milgram's 'Behavioral Study of Obedience' (1963), Skinner's 'The Behavior of Organisms' (1938), Piaget's 'The Construction of Reality in the Child' (1954), Maslow's 'A Theory of Human Motivation' (1943), Vygotsky's 'Mind in Society' (1978), Erikson's 'Childhood and Society' (1950), Bowlby's 'Attachment and Loss Vol. 1' (1969), and Pavlov's 'Conditioned Reflexes' (1927).
